author: ivanlitteri
content: Haha, I'm doing dummy proofs rn, I'll try to plug a proper prover worker.

author: taulepton_
content: cool sounds good. Regarding the prover, are you spawning that directly, or do you plan to use the ere / zkboost integration?

author: ivanlitteri
content: I'm planning on doing ere/zkboost integration. Our first implementation of EIP-8025 (https://x.com/ethrex_client/status/2037929626316472493?s=20) used a local proof engine and proof coordination logic. These are not really needed in the EL thanks to zkboost; nevertheless it was faster to do it that way at first because we inherited that logic from our L2 crate

author: taulepton_
content: Right, I was a little bit confused as to why your ethrex client had implemented the proof engine API. Can you clarify whether your intention is to use the ethrex as a node client to provide zbkoost with the witnesses' data, or if you plan to integrate some proof system as well?

author: ivanlitteri
content: For now, I want to use zkboost. The only reason I'd have a proof system integrated as well would be as a fallback mechanism (but I need to think better if it's worth the code complexity, using zkboost cut ~3k lines of code)

author: taulepton_
content: Can’t you clarify what’s you’re integrating. Is it just ethrex or something else (a custom prover?)

author: taulepton_
content: ahh ok got it. I think in that case you can just replace reth with ethrex in the kurtosis config I provided.
